item.title: Adaptive Estimation of Unknown Road Terrain
item.description: The primary objective of this project was to adaptively estimate the road profile from the motion of a quarter-car model. The equations of motions for a quarter-car model moving on a road profile were first derived. The unknown road profile was represented as the sum of unknown coefficients time basis functions. I used linear finite element basis functions. The unknown coefficients were determined using gradient law as the learning law. The above problem was modelled in Matlab and the estimated road profile matched the actual profile.

item.title: Nonlinear Control of Robotic Manipulator
item.description: Designed and simulationed (using MATLAB) sliding mode and backstepping controllers for a serial robotic manipulator.

item.title: Multibody Dynamics Library for 2D and 3D Multibody Systems
item.description: The primary motivation to develop a multibody dynamics library is different systems with different parameters can be simulated using the same library. This eliminates the need for specialized codes for individual problems. For example, different codes have to be developed for the simulation of a double pendulum and an excavator. But, once the library is developed, both the systems can be simulated using the same library. The library was developed using Matlab. This project gave an intuition about how multibody dynamics simulation softwares like Adams work.

item.title: Semi-Autonomous Robot Development
item.description: Implemented Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) Algorithm for a camera equipped robot operating in remote location.
